json — Conversion to and from json¶

New in version 0.1.

Module author: Claude Gibert <claude.gibert@synopticview.co.uk>

The Json class¶

class core.json.JSon(object)¶

The JSon class only wraps the json (or simplejson) module’s dump, dumps, load and loads functions. It ensures that:

  • floats are represented properly and there is no accuracy problem (see for example http://tinyurl.com/ykhqrre)
  • when decoding a json string, the standard module creates unicode strings. These cannot be used for the name of variables before python 3.0, so at the moment, JSon convert all unicodes strings to python strs.
classmethod encode(destination = None, **kargs)¶
Parameters:
  • destination (stream) – optional, if specified, a stream to encode into, if not, encoding is made in a string which is returned.
  • kargs (names parameters) – parameters to encode into json (encoded as a dictionary)
classmethod decode(source)¶
Parameters:source (either a stream (e.g. file) or a string) – source to decode from

Example:

from semperpy.core.json import JSon

s = JSon.encode(data={'hello':'world'},hello="world")
print s
{"data": {"hello": "world"}, "hello": "world"}
print JSon.decode(s)
{'data': {'hello': 'world'}, 'hello': 'world'}
